title: pelargonium × floccosum
intro: Pelargonium × floccosum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Prune Geranium, is a perennial plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Pelargonium × floccosum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties and is often used in traditional African and Western herbal remedies.
